concept: Complete / Having All Parts / Whole and Finished
description: Having all necessary parts, elements, or steps; entire and whole. 'Sholu' names the quality of completeness—nothing missing, nothing more needed.
sound_symbolism: The word finishes fully. 'Sho' /ʃo/ gathers with soft certainty, parts assembling. Then 'lu' settles into wholeness, completion achieved.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'sholu telo' (complete work). Related to 'whoa' (whole) but emphasizes having all parts rather than undivided unity.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Complete crafts needed nothing added—traditional work achieved fullness through sufficiency.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Each moment is complete—nothing needs to be added for this moment to be fully itself.
